review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to managing service accounts and user access, which can help meet the NIST control requirement of disabling identifiers after inactivity. The function names mostly follow conventions, but one was slightly modified for clarity. The functions map well to Kubernetes security practices, though Kubernetes does not directly support disabling identifiers after inactivity, managing service account tokens and reviewing user access policies are appropriate measures.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'scheduled' instead of 'implemented' to better reflect the periodic nature of access reviews.
title: 3_5_6: 3.5.6 Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity
step1_reasoning: The control 3.5.6 requires disabling identifiers after a period of inactivity. In Kubernetes, this can translate to managing service accounts and user access. Ensuring service account tokens have expiration and reviewing user access policies can help meet this requirement. While Kubernetes does not have a direct equivalent to password expiration, managing service account tokens and user access policies can achieve similar security outcomes.
